result:
Done. The X/Twitter DM channel extension is complete at `extensions/x-twitter/`. Here's what was created:

**12 files, 13 passing tests:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `package.json` | Plugin manifest with `@openclaw/x-twitter` name and channel metadata |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin registration |
| `index.ts` | Entry point - registers channel plugin via `api.registerChannel()` |
| `src/runtime.ts` | Singleton runtime accessor |
| `src/types.ts` | `XTwitterConfig`, `XTwitterDmConfig`, `CoreConfig` types |
| `src/config-schema.ts` | Zod validation schema for config |
| `src/x-api/client.ts` | X API v2 client (`xApiFetch`, `getAuthenticatedUser`) |
| `src/x-api/probe.ts` | Health check via `GET /2/users/me` |
| `src/x-api/send.ts` | Send DMs via `POST /2/dm_conversations/with/:id/messages` |
| `src/x-api/monitor.ts` | Polling-based inbound DM monitoring via `GET /2/dm_events` |
| `src/x-api/accounts.ts` | Multi-account resolution (list/resolve/auth) |
| `src/channel.ts` | Full `ChannelPlugin` contract (meta, pairing, capabilities, config, security, directory, messaging, actions, setup, outbound, status, gateway) |
| `src/outbound.ts` | `ChannelOutboundAdapter` for sending text + media |
| `src/actions.ts` | `ChannelMessageActionAdapter` with send/read actions |
| `src/onboarding.ts` | CLI wizard (env var detection, credential prompts, allowFrom) |
| `src/channel.test.ts` | 13 unit tests covering metadata, capabilities, directory, normalization, DM policy, config resolution, and actions |
